rules.trigger: Don't clone when borrowing works. Trigger when working on anti-patterns and the code shows `anti`-class risk.
Don't collect intermediate iterators. Trigger when working on anti-patterns and the code shows `anti`-class risk.
Don't silently ignore errors. Trigger when working on anti-patterns and the code shows `anti`-class risk.
Don't use expect for recoverable errors. Trigger when working on anti-patterns and the code shows `anti`-class risk.
Don't use format! in hot paths. Trigger when working on anti-patterns and the code shows `anti`-class risk.
Don't use indexing when iterators work. Trigger when working on anti-patterns and the code shows `anti`-class risk.
Don't hold locks across await points. Trigger when working on anti-patterns and the code shows `anti`-class risk.
Don't over-abstract with excessive generics. Trigger when working on anti-patterns and the code shows `anti`-class risk.
Don't panic on expected or recoverable errors. Trigger when working on anti-patterns and the code shows `anti`-class risk.
Don't optimize before profiling. Trigger when working on anti-patterns and the code shows `anti`-class risk.
Don't accept &String when &str works. Trigger when working on anti-patterns and the code shows `anti`-class risk.
Don't use strings where enums or newtypes would provide type safety. Trigger when working on anti-patterns and the code shows `anti`-class risk.

Don't accept &Vec<T> when &[T] works. Trigger when working on anti-patterns and the code shows `anti`-class risk.
Mark builder methods with `#[must_use]` to prevent silent drops. Trigger when working on public API design and the code shows `api`-class risk.
Use Builder pattern for complex construction. Trigger when working on public API design and the code shows `api`-class risk.

Constructors, builders, or factory functions that receive user-owned strings, paths, byte buffers, or domain values.

Use extension traits to add methods to external types. Trigger when working on public API design and the code shows `api`-class risk.

Parse into validated types at boundaries. Trigger when working on public API design and the code shows `api`-class risk.

Use `AsyncFn`/`AsyncFnMut`/`AsyncFnOnce` bounds instead of `F: Fn() -> Fut, Fut: Future`. Trigger when working on async runtime and cancellation and the code shows `async`-class risk.
Async functions containing synchronous sleep, filesystem, compression, parsing, crypto, CPU-bound loops, or blocking client calls.
Use bounded channels to apply backpressure and prevent unbounded memory growth. Trigger when working on async runtime and cancellation and the code shows `async`-class risk.
Use `broadcast` channel for pub/sub where all subscribers receive all messages. Trigger when working on async runtime and cancellation and the code shows `async`-class risk.

Clone Arc/Rc data before await points to avoid holding references across suspension. Trigger when working on async runtime and cancellation and the code shows `async`-class risk.

Capture only what you use; lean on edition-2021 disjoint closure captures. Trigger when working on closures and callbacks and the code shows `closure`-class risk.

Require the least restrictive `Fn` trait a callback needs (`FnOnce` superset-or-equal `FnMut` superset-or-equal `Fn`). Trigger when working on closures and callbacks and the code shows `closure`-class risk.
Return closures as `impl Fn`/`FnMut`/`FnOnce`, not `Box<dyn Fn>`. Trigger when working on closures and callbacks and the code shows `closure`-class risk.
Use `move` for closures that outlive the current scope; clone before `move` to keep the original. Trigger when working on closures and callbacks and the code shows `closure`-class risk.

Use `BinaryHeap` for a priority queue or repeated max-extraction. Trigger when working on collections and the code shows `coll`-class risk.

Pick the map by access pattern: `HashMap` (fast, unordered), `BTreeMap` (sorted / range queries), `IndexMap` (insertion order). Trigger when working on collections and the code shows `coll`-class risk.
Default to `Vec`; use `VecDeque` for queue/deque behaviour; avoid `LinkedList`. Trigger when working on collections and the code shows `coll`-class risk.
Use `HashSet`/`BTreeSet` for membership tests and dedup, not linear `Vec::contains`. Trigger when working on collections and the code shows `coll`-class risk.

Use an associated type when each impl has exactly one output type; use a generic parameter when a type can implement the trait for many input types. Trigger when working on traits and generics and the code shows `trait`-class risk.
Use a blanket impl `impl<T: Bound> Trait for T` to give behaviour to every type that satisfies a bound. Trigger when working on traits and generics and the code shows `trait`-class risk.
Respect the orphan rule; wrap a foreign type in a newtype to implement a foreign trait on it. Trigger when working on traits and generics and the code shows `trait`-class risk.
Define a trait in terms of a few required methods plus defaulted ones built on top of them. Trigger when working on traits and generics and the code shows `trait`-class risk.
Choose static dispatch (generics / `impl Trait`) vs dynamic dispatch (`dyn Trait`) deliberately. Trigger when working on traits and generics and the code shows `trait`-class risk.
Keep a trait dyn-compatible (object-safe) when you need `dyn Trait`. Trigger when working on traits and generics and the code shows `trait`-class risk.

In Rust 2024, wrap `extern` blocks in `unsafe extern { }` and annotate each item as `safe` or `unsafe`.. Trigger when working on unsafe soundness and the code shows `unsafe`-class risk.

Use `MaybeUninit<T>` for uninitialized memory; never use `mem::uninitialized()` or `mem::zeroed()` for types with validity invariants.. Trigger when working on unsafe soundness and the code shows `unsafe`-class risk.

Keep `unsafe` blocks as small as possible - mark only the operation that requires unsafety, not the surrounding safe code.. Trigger when working on unsafe soundness and the code shows `unsafe`-class risk.
Run `cargo miri test` in CI for every crate that contains `unsafe` code.. Trigger when working on unsafe soundness and the code shows `unsafe`-class risk.

In Rust 2024, write `#[unsafe(no_mangle)]`, `#[unsafe(export_name = "...")]`, and `#[unsafe(link_section = "...")]` - not the bare attribute forms.. Trigger when working on unsafe soundness and the code shows `unsafe`-class risk.
Write a `// SAFETY:` comment above every `unsafe` block and a `# Safety` section in every `unsafe fn`.. Trigger when working on unsafe soundness and the code shows `unsafe`-class risk.
